summary: Spectrum peaks were binned into 10,000 equally-sized bins ranging from 10 to 1000 m/z. The input to the neural network is the binned spectrum vector ranging from 10 to 1000 m/z with up to 10,000 bins.
The dropout rate used in the neural network is 0.2.
The network uses L1 and L2 weight regularization with value 10^-6 in the first dense layer.
For Monte-Carlo Dropout ensembles, N=10 embeddings were created from networks with dropout enabled.
The neural network was trained using the Adam optimizer with a learning rate of 0.001.
A batch size of 32 was used during model training.
Training continued until the validation loss did not decrease for 5 epochs using early stopping.
The base network creates abstract embeddings with 200-dimensional vectors.
The first two densely connected layers each have 500 nodes.
Low-intensity peak removal data augmentation randomly removes non-zero bins with intensity below 0.4 in 0-20% of cases.
Peak intensity jitter data augmentation applies changes between 0% and 40% to non-zero bin intensities.
New peak addition data augmentation adds 0-10 randomly selected peaks with intensities between 0 and 0.01.
MS2DeepScore is implemented as a Siamese neural network with two components: a base network and a head part with cosine calculation.
Peak intensities were square root transformed to avoid too strong a focus on the highest intensity peaks.
The maximum number of peaks retained per spectrum was limited to 1,000 highest intensity peaks.
Each training epoch uses one cycle of data generation that goes through all spectra in the training set once.
Structural similarity labels are distributed into 10 equally-sized bins between 0 and 1 for balanced sampling.
The validation set uses 10 cycles, iterating 10 times over 3,597 spectra to monitor training progress on 35,970 total spectrum pairs.
